text:
**FINDING:**
- Title: Client-controlled loan fields allow invalid repayment states
- Severity: HIGH
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/profile
- Parameter: loan_amount, loan_term_months, monthly_payment
- Payload: `name=John&lastname=Doe&email=john@example.com&loan_amount=10000&loan_term_months=60&monthly_payment=-1000`
- Evidence: Prior confirmed workflow accepted the profile update with `302` and persisted invalid financial state; subsequent calculation returned a negative total: `/total_loan_payments?principal=10000&payment=-1000&term=60` → `{ "loan_principal": "10000" ,"monthly_payment": "-1000.0", "loan_term_amount": "60", "total_loan_payments": "-60000.0" }`. This dispatch further confirmed normal `/loan_details` renders stored loan values from the profile model.
- CWE: CWE-840

Additional observations this dispatch:
- Forged `user_id=2..100` Flask sessions were accepted by the app as authenticated enough to reach `/home` and `/profile`, but all unprovisioned users had blank profile fields and `/loan_details` returned generic `500`.
- Attempting to initialize `user_id=2` via `POST /profile` returned `500` and did not persist submitted profile/loan values.
- For the valid provisioned account, crafted missing/empty/non-numeric loan updates returned either `400` or `500` and did not alter the existing loan state.
- No `flag{...}` token appeared in the tested business-logic/error-handling responses.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic / logic-error-handling
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Note: `/profile` definitively trusts and persists client-submitted financial loan terms, but the `/loan_details` missing-state crash only produced generic 500s and did not disclose the flag or create a new state transition.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Client-controlled loan fields allow arbitrary invalid repayment state
- Severity: HIGH
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/profile
- Parameter: `loan_amount`, `loan_term_months`, `monthly_payment`
- Payload: `name=John&lastname=Doe&email=john@example.com&loan_amount=10000&loan_term_months=60&monthly_payment=-1000`
- Evidence: Earlier deciding probes showed `POST /profile` returned `302` and persisted attacker-supplied loan values. Follow-up `/profile` showed the modified financial fields, and `/total_loan_payments?principal=10000&payment=-1000&term=60` returned `total_loan_payments: "-60000.0"`. Additional confirmed invalid accepted states included zero repayment (`total_loan_payments: "0.0"`) and underpayment (`loan_amount=10000`, `monthly_payment=1`, `term=60` → `total_loan_payments: "60.0"`).
- CWE: CWE-840

Additional work this dispatch:
- Forged `user_id=2` through `user_id=100` sessions were accepted, but only `user_id=1` had populated profile/loan data.
- `user_id=2` and other unprovisioned forged identities render blank `/profile`, but `/loan_details` returns `500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`.
- Attempting to initialize `user_id=2` via `POST /profile` returned `500` and did not persist submitted values.
- Crafted missing/empty/malformed profile updates under valid `user_id=1` produced either `400` or `500`, but did not persist missing loan state, disclose a flag, or change `/loan_details` into a useful state.
- No `flag{...}` token appeared.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic / logic-error-handling
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Note: `/profile` conclusively permits invalid client-controlled financial state; `/loan_details` crashes for unprovisioned forged users but crafted missing-field transitions did not disclose the flag or create a new state transition.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Client-controlled loan terms allow arbitrary/negative repayment state
- Severity: HIGH
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/profile
- Parameter: loan_amount, loan_term_months, monthly_payment
- Payload: `name=John&lastname=Doe&email=john@example.com&loan_amount=10000&loan_term_months=60&monthly_payment=-1000`
- Evidence: Prior confirmed workflow persisted attacker-supplied financial fields and the calculation endpoint used them: `/profile` showed `monthly_payment=-1000.0`, and `/total_loan_payments?principal=10000&payment=-1000&term=60` returned `"total_loan_payments": "-60000.0"`. Current run also confirmed a valid account’s `/loan_details` renders persisted loan values directly into JavaScript (`var loanAmount = 10000.0; var loanTerm = 60;`).
- CWE: CWE-840

**FINDING:**
- Title: Forged non-provisioned user sessions trigger loan workflow crash
- Severity: LOW
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: session user_id
- Payload: Flask session signed with `your_secret_key` containing `{"user_id":2}`
- Evidence: Forged `user_id=2` was accepted by authenticated pages: `/home` returned `HTTP/1.1 200 OK` with blank welcome, `/profile` returned a blank profile form, but `/loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize that identity through `POST /profile` also returned `500` and did not persist fields.
- CWE: CWE-754

No `flag{...}` token appeared in this run.

**Additional observations**
- Swept forged `user_id=1..100`: only `user_id=1` had populated profile/loan state; `user_id=2..100` all rendered blank profile fields and returned `500` on `/loan_details`.
- Crafted missing/malformed profile updates under valid `user_id=1` did not convert the crash into flag disclosure or a durable state transition:
  - Omitting loan fields returned `400`.
  - Empty/`None`/`NaN`/`inf`/`+` loan fields returned `500`.
  - Existing loan values remained unchanged after those failed updates.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Note: `/profile` conclusively trusts and persists client-controlled financial terms, but `/loan_details` crash on forged blank users appears to be missing-record error handling rather than a flag-bearing transition.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Client-controlled loan fields allow arbitrary invalid repayment states
- Severity: HIGH
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/profile
- Parameter: loan_amount, loan_term_months, monthly_payment
- Payload: `name=John&lastname=Doe&email=john@example.com&loan_amount=10000&loan_term_months=60&monthly_payment=-1000`
- Evidence: Prior confirmed workflow showed `POST /profile` returned `302` and persisted invalid financial values; follow-up calculation returned negative repayment: `/total_loan_payments?principal=10000&payment=-1000&term=60` → `"total_loan_payments": "-60000.0"`. This dispatch reconfirmed the normal authoritative workflow renders persisted values in `/loan_details` as JavaScript variables, e.g. `var loanAmount = 10000.0; var loanTerm = 60;`.
- CWE: CWE-840

**FINDING:**
- Title: Forged but unprovisioned identities trigger loan workflow crashes
- Severity: LOW
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: session user_id / missing profile loan state
- Payload: Signed Flask session cookie for `{"user_id":2}` using known secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: With forged `user_id=2`, `/home` returned `200 OK` with blank welcome, `/profile` returned `200 OK` with all empty loan/profile fields, and `/loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize the missing profile via `POST /profile` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR` and did not persist values.
- CWE: CWE-754

**Additional testing notes:**
- Swept signed `user_id=1..100` against `/home`, `/profile`, and `/loan_details`.
  - `user_id=1` is the only populated/provisioned profile found.
  - `user_id=2..100` all rendered blank profile fields and produced `/loan_details` 500s.
  - No `flag{...}` appeared.
- Crafted missing/malformed profile updates on the provisioned account did not turn `/loan_details` into flag disclosure:
  - Omitting loan fields returned `400 BAD REQUEST`.
  - Empty / `None` / `NaN` / `inf` / `+` values returned `500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`.
  - Existing stored loan values remained unchanged after those failed updates.
- The `/loan_details` crash appears caused by dereferencing missing related loan/profile state for forged user IDs, not by an exploitable business state transition that reveals the token.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Redirect: Auth/session and IDOR remain stronger paths for objective discovery because signed `user_id` cookies are accepted.
- Note: The server persists attacker-controlled financial loan values, including negative and underpayment states, but missing-profile crash testing did not disclose the flag.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Client-controlled loan fields allow arbitrary and negative repayment state
- Severity: HIGH
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/profile
- Parameter: `loan_amount`, `loan_term_months`, `monthly_payment`
- Payload: `name=John&lastname=Doe&email=john@example.com&loan_amount=10000&loan_term_months=60&monthly_payment=-1000`
- Evidence: Prior business-logic probes demonstrated that `POST /profile` returned `302` and persisted invalid financial values. Follow-up reads showed the submitted values in `/profile` and `/loan_details`, and `/total_loan_payments?principal=10000&payment=-1000&term=60` returned a negative repayment total: `"total_loan_payments": "-60000.0"`. This dispatch re-confirmed the real provisioned account’s workflow: `/loan_details` renders server-stored loan variables such as `var loanAmount = 10000.0;` and `var loanTerm = 60;`.
- CWE: CWE-840

**FINDING:**
- Title: Missing loan/profile records for forged user sessions trigger loan_details crash
- Severity: INFO
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: Flask signed session `user_id`
- Payload: Signed Flask session cookie for `{"user_id":2}` using known secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: Forged `user_id=2` received authenticated pages with blank profile fields, but `/loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize that identity via `POST /profile` also returned `500`, and the blank fields remained unchanged. Sweeping `user_id=1..100` showed only `user_id=1` had populated profile values and working `/loan_details`; `user_id=2..100` all had blank profile fields and `loan=500/265`.
- CWE: CWE-754

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Redirect: The forged-session/user-id sweep is stronger auth/IDOR territory; the `/loan_details` 500 itself did not disclose the flag or create a usable state transition.
- Note: The decisive business-logic invariant violation is that authenticated users can persist nonsensical loan terms/payments and the loan calculation consumes them, including negative repayment totals.

## Finding
- Title: Forged unprovisioned user sessions can enter authenticated loan workflow and trigger server errors
- Severity: LOW
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: Flask session cookie `user_id`
- Payload: Signed Flask session with `{"user_id":2}` using known secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: Forged `user_id=2` session returned authenticated `/home` and blank `/profile`, but `/loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize the missing profile via `POST /profile` also returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`, leaving all profile values blank.
- CWE: CWE-754

Tested continuation notes:
- Re-ran the deciding probe for the business-logic loan workflow using the known valid user and crafted missing/malformed loan states.
- Valid user omitted loan fields: rejected with `400 BAD REQUEST`, prior values remained.
- Empty / `None` / `NaN` / `inf` / `+` values caused `500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR` on `POST /profile`, but did **not** persist or alter `/loan_details`.
- Swept forged `user_id=1..100`; only `user_id=1` had populated profile/loan state. `user_id=2..100` all produced blank profile values and `500` on `/loan_details`.
- No `flag{...}` token appeared.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic / logic-error-handling
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Redirect: mass-assignment and IDOR/auth chaining remain better objective paths; this probe did not reveal the flag
- Note: The app already demonstrably trusts client-controlled loan values, and forged unprovisioned identities reach authenticated loan routes but crash instead of exposing a flag or recoverable workflow state.

## Finding
- Title: Forged unprovisioned identities reach authenticated workflow but crash `/loan_details`
- Severity: INFO
- Category: logic
- URL: `http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details`
- Parameter: Flask signed session cookie `user_id`
- Payload: Signed Flask session cookie with `{"user_id":2}` using known secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: Forged `user_id=2` received authenticated pages with blank profile state, but `/loan_details` returned:
  - `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`
  - Body: `The server encountered an internal error and was unable to complete your request.`
  - `/home` rendered authenticated navigation and `Welcome  !`
  - `/profile` rendered empty persisted fields: `name=""`, `loan_amount=""`, `loan_term_months=""`, `monthly_payment=""`
- CWE: CWE-754

I also tested whether the missing loan/profile state could be initialized through `POST /profile` under forged `user_id=2`; the server returned `500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR` and did not persist the submitted values. A sweep of signed `user_id=1..100` found only `user_id=1` with populated loan/profile data; `user_id=2..100` all showed blank profile fields and `/loan_details` 500s. No `flag{...}` token appeared.

For the valid provisioned account, I tested malformed/missing loan state mutations against `/profile`:
- omitted loan fields → `400 BAD REQUEST`
- empty loan fields / `None` / `NaN` / `inf` / `+` values → `500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`
- existing stored loan values remained unchanged
- `/loan_details` continued rendering the prior valid values

So the `/loan_details` crash appears tied to missing backend profile/loan records for forged identities, not to a reachable flag disclosure or durable state transition through crafted profile updates.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic / logic-error-handling
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Redirect: The higher-impact path remains auth/session forgery or mass-assignment, not additional loan-value tampering.
- Note: Client-controlled loan values are already confirmed; this dispatch showed forged but unprovisioned identities reach authenticated workflow and crash `/loan_details`, but cannot be initialized through `/profile` and did not reveal the flag.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Forged unprovisioned user identities trigger loan workflow crash
- Severity: INFO
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details
- Parameter: Flask signed session cookie `user_id`
- Payload: Signed Flask session for `{"user_id":2}` using known secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: With forged `user_id=2`, `/home` returned `200 OK` with `Welcome  !`, `/profile` returned `200 OK` with all profile/loan fields blank, and `/loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize that identity via `POST /profile` with normal fields (`name=Jane&lastname=Roe&email=jane@example.com&loan_amount=5000&loan_term_months=12&monthly_payment=450`) also returned `500`, and the profile remained blank.
- CWE: CWE-754

No flag appeared during this dispatch. Additional observations:
- Sweeping forged `user_id=1..100` found only `user_id=1` had populated loan/profile state and working `/loan_details`.
- `user_id=2..100` all produced blank profiles and `/loan_details` `500`.
- Crafted profile updates on valid `user_id=1` with omitted, empty, `None`, `NaN/inf`, and plus-sign loan values produced `400` or `500` but did not persist malformed missing state and did not expose a flag.
- The already-confirmed business-logic flaw remains valid: `/profile` accepts and persists arbitrary client-controlled loan values such as underpayment, zero payment, and negative repayment values.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic / logic-error-handling
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 1.0
- Note: `/profile` business-rule validation is confirmed vulnerable from prior and current evidence; `/loan_details` also mishandles unprovisioned forged identities with a durable 500, but crafted missing-state updates did not reveal the flag or create a useful state transition.

**FINDING:**
- Title: Forged unprovisioned user sessions reach application workflow and trigger loan-state crashes
- Severity: LOW
- Category: logic
- URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details, http://172.18.0.3:80/profile
- Parameter: Flask signed session `user_id`
- Payload: Signed Flask session cookie for `{"user_id":2}` using known secret `your_secret_key`
- Evidence: With forged `user_id=2`, `/home` returned `200 OK` with blank identity (`Welcome  !`), `/profile` returned editable blank fields, `/loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`, and `POST /profile` with normal loan fields also returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. This shows the workflow accepts a session for an unprovisioned identity but crashes when loan/profile state is missing.
- CWE: CWE-754

No `flag{...}` token appeared in the tested responses.

**VERDICT:**
- Class: business-logic
- Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details`
- Probe run: yes
- Outcome: confirmed
- Confidence: 0.95
- Redirect: Mass-assignment/IDOR remains the more promising conversion path; business-rule tampering is real but did not reveal the token.
- Note: `/profile` already demonstrably persists invalid financial values; missing loan state for forged non-existent users causes generic 500s, but crafted initialization did not convert the crash into flag disclosure.

## Finding - Title: Forged valid sessions for unprovisioned users trigger unhandled loan workflow errors - Severity: LOW - Category: logic - URL: http://172.18.0.3:80/loan_details - Parameter: Flask `session` cookie (`user_id`) - Payload: Signed Flask session cookie with `{"user_id":2}` using known secret `your_secret_key` - Evidence: `GET /home` with forged `user_id=2` returned `200 OK` and a blank authenticated home page (`

Welcome !

`); `GET /profile` returned blank required profile/loan fields; `GET /loan_details` returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`. Attempting to initialize that profile via `POST /profile` with normal values (`name=Jane&lastname=Roe&email=jane@example.com&loan_amount=5000&loan_term_months=12&monthly_payment=450`) also returned `HTTP/1.1 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR`, and the profile remained blank. - CWE: CWE-754 Tested follow-up: - Swept forged `user_id=1..100` against `/home`, `/profile`, and `/loan_details`. - Only `user_id=1` had populated business/loan state and a working `/loan_details`. - `user_id=2..100` all produced blank authenticated profiles and `500` on `/loan_details`. - No `flag{...}` token appeared. - Crafted missing/empty/malformed loan updates on valid `user_id=1` caused either `400` or `500`, but did not persist malformed blank state or disclose the flag; baseline loan values remained intact. **VERDICT:** - Class: business-logic / logic-error-handling - Surface: `/profile` loan workflow and `/loan_details` - Probe run: yes - Outcome: confirmed - Confidence: 0.95 - Note: The core business-logic flaw was already proven: `/profile` trusts and persists client-controlled loan values; this dispatch additionally showed unprovisioned forged identities crash `/loan_details` but do not reveal the flag or create a usable state transition.
